{{other uses}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Oi Shrine |image = [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |location = [[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]] Oimachi 2316 |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|1.18|N|138|10|18.40|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}}
{{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|ハニヤス|12=draft}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} |shrine_rank = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}
{{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} |founded = Unknown |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} |alternate_names = |offices = |annual_festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|12=draft}} |rituals = {{ill|Shimada Grand festival|ja|島田大祭|12=draft}} (Held every three years) }} [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Oi Shrine''' (大井神社, Oi-jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}}, a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}, and currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is dedicated to the spirit of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} and is famous for the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り) held every three years. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history. * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}} (Mizuhanome no Kami) - deity of water. * {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|ハニヤス|12=draft}} (Haniyasu Hime no Kami) - deity of earth. * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} (Amaterasu Sume Ohmikami) - deity of the sun. In historical texts, the shrine is referred to as "Oi no Kami," indicating its origins in the deification of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}}. The three deities mentioned above were enshrined later, particularly Mizuhanome, who reflects the shrine's original connection to water.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} == History == [[File:Oigawa Shimada.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} (Ancient Ritual Site)}}]] [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] == Overview == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Historical records indicate that in 865 (Jōgan 7), the deity of "Oi no Kami" in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国|12=draft}} was elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade. However, the shrine is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} records, making it a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}} (a shrine not listed in the Engishiki). The {{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|12=draft}} records indicate that "Oi Tenjin" had a divine rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} According to tradition, the shrine was originally located in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} in the village of Yahatamura (now part of {{ill|Kawanehon, Shizuoka|ja|川根本町|12=draft}}) but was swept away by a flood in 1276 (Kenji 2) and drifted to Shimada, where it has since been enshrined at its current location.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} A monument now marks the original site of the shrine in the Yahatamura area, although there are several other locations with similar claims.[http://www.ooijinjya.org/knowledge/knowledge03.html Knowledge about the Oi Shrine] (Official website). Due to the frequent flooding of the Oigawa River, over 50 branch shrines of "Oi Shrine" have been established throughout the region.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} During the [[Edo period]], the shrine was temporarily relocated to the Gotemizutani area of Nodamura in 1604 (Keicho 9) due to the collapse of the Oigawa River embankment, but it was returned to its original location in 1615 (Genna 1).{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} In 1689 (Genroku 2), the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the development of {{ill|Shimada-juku|ja|島田宿|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} The shrine received a small donation of land from {{ill|Imagawa Ujizane|ja|今川氏真|12=draft}}, and during the {{ill|Tokugawa|ja|徳川氏|12=draft}} period, it had an exemption of 5 bushels and 9 ho.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1875 and promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1908. In 1966 (Showa 41), it was designated a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. === Divine rankings === * Historical records during the Six National Histories period ** 865 (Jōgan 7) December 21, elevated from Junior Sixth rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}) - Recorded as "Oi no Kami." * Post-Six National Histories ** Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|12=draft}}) - Recorded as "Oi Tenjin." == Sub-Shrines == The shrine grounds contain the following five sub-shrines: * Oi Tenmangu ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} ** festival: October 25 * Kasuga Shrine ** Deities: Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, Futsunushi no Mikoto, and nine others ** festival: Early June * Oi Ebisu Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft}} ** festival: November 19 * Haraedo Shrine ** Deities: Haraedo no Ōkami (four deities) ** festival: May 5 * Shizutama Shrine ** Deities: Over 1950 martyrs from Shimada ** festival: April 15 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Kasuga-jinja & Tenmangu.JPG|Oi Tenmangu (right) and Kasuga Shrine (left) File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Ebisu-jinja.JPG|Oi Ebisu Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Haraedo-jinja.JPG|Haraedo Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Shizutama-jinja.JPG|Shizutama Shrine == annual Events == === festivals === The major festivals held at Oi Shrine include:[http://www.ooijinjya.org/events/index.html annual Events] (Official website). * Saitan-sai (January 1) * Setsubun-sai (February 3) * Hina Doll Burning festival (April 7) * Kasuga Shrine festival (June 5) * Misogi no Harae (June 30) * Summer festival, Tanabata festival (August 1) * festival (October 15) * Ebisu Shrine festival, Ebisu Kosha festival (November 19) * Chinka-sai (December 1) === Shimada Grand festival === The shrine's festival is known as the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り). It is said to have started in 1695 (Genroku 8) and is held every three years (years of the Tiger, Snake, Monkey, and Boar) over three days in mid-October. The festival is particularly famous for the daimyo procession on the final day. This procession recreates the grandeur of the Genroku period, with retainers carrying a childbirth prayer sash (the origin of the festival's name) on wooden swords, along with the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊|12=draft}} dance for pestilence avoidance and kabuki dances by children on {{ill|Yatai|ja|屋台|12=draft}} floats. During the festival, Shimada's central area is divided into seven districts, with different events held in each: nagauta (long song) festivals with floats in districts 1-5, Kashima Odori in district 6, and the daimyo procession and parade in district 7. The {{ill|Nenchū Gyōji Jiten|ja|年中行事辞典|12=draft}} lists the [[Yoshida Fire festival|Yoshida Fire festival]] in Yamanashi, the Shimada Grand festival, and the [[Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri|Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri]] in Aichi as the "Three Great Strange festivals of Japan." ‘Second Exhibition: Shimada Grand festival’, Shimada City Museum, 1992, p.6 Additionally, the daimyo procession of the Shimada Grand festival and the Kashima Odori dance are designated as Intangible Folk Cultural Properties by Shizuoka Prefecture. == Cultural Properties == === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** {{ill|Shimada Kashima Odori|ja|島田鹿島踊|12=draft}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on May 13, 1957.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/kashima.html Shimada Kashima Odori] (Shimada City Museum website). ** {{ill|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|ja|島田帯祭の大名行列|12=draft}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on March 12, 1996.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/obimatsuri-daimyou.html Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession] (Shimada City Museum website). == location Information == '''location''' * Oimachi 2316, Shimada, Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan '''Access''' * Train: 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shimada Station|ja|島田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft}} of {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} (JR Tōkai) '''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|ja|島田宿大井川川越遺跡|12=draft}} - National Historic Site. * {{ill|Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|ja|蓬萊橋|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine history * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/885}} '府県郷社明治神社誌料 上'] (National Diet Library Digital Collections) See 885-886 frames. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref=Shimada City History|1978th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=野本寛一|authorlink=野本寛一|year=1987|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=白水社|isbn=4560022208|ref=Oi Shrine (Gods)|1987th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Shimada-juku|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref=Shimada-juku (Heibonsha)|2000th year }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} * [http://www.ooijinjya.org/ Oi Shrine] - Official website * {{Portal Regional Culture Assets|22_shizuoka|7277|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession (video)}} {{Shinto-navbox}} {{Normdaten}} {{Izanami-firebirths}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimada]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shimada]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Oi Shrine]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oi Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Oi Shrine]] [[sn:Oi Shrine]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BA%95%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B3%B6%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[sn:i Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Shrine}}

Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}

However, the shrine is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} records, making it a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}} (a shrine not listed in the Engishiki). The {{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|12=draft}} records indicate that "Oi Tenjin" had a divine rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} According to tradition, the shrine was originally located in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} in the village of Yahatamura (now part of {{ill|Kawanehon, Shizuoka|ja|川根本町|12=draft}}) but was swept away by a flood in 1276 (Kenji 2) and drifted to Shimada, where it has since been enshrined at its current location.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} A monument now marks the original site of the shrine in the Yahatamura area, although there are several other locations with similar claims.[http://www.ooijinjya.org/knowledge/knowledge03.html Knowledge about the Oi Shrine] (Official website). Due to the frequent flooding of the Oigawa River, over 50 branch shrines of "Oi Shrine" have been established throughout the region.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} During the [[Edo period]], the shrine was temporarily relocated to the Gotemizutani area of Nodamura in 1604 (Keicho 9) due to the collapse of the Oigawa River embankment, but it was returned to its original location in 1615 (Genna 1).{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} In 1689 (Genroku 2), the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the development of {{ill|Shimada-juku|ja|島田宿|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} The shrine received a small donation of land from {{ill|Imagawa Ujizane|ja|今川氏真|12=draft}}, and during the {{ill|Tokugawa|ja|徳川氏|12=draft}} period, it had an exemption of 5 bushels and 9 ho.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1875 and promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1908. In 1966 (Showa 41), it was designated a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. === Divine rankings === * Historical records during the Six National Histories period ** 865 (Jōgan 7) December 21, elevated from Junior Sixth rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}) - Recorded as "Oi no Kami." * Post-Six National Histories ** Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|12=draft}}) - Recorded as "Oi Tenjin." == Sub-Shrines == The shrine grounds contain the following five sub-shrines: * Oi Tenmangu ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} ** festival: October 25 * Kasuga Shrine ** Deities: Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, Futsunushi no Mikoto, and nine others ** festival: Early June * Oi Ebisu Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft}} ** festival: November 19 * Haraedo Shrine ** Deities: Haraedo no Ōkami (four deities) ** festival: May 5 * Shizutama Shrine ** Deities: Over 1950 martyrs from Shimada ** festival: April 15 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Kasuga-jinja & Tenmangu.JPG|Oi Tenmangu (right) and Kasuga Shrine (left) File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Ebisu-jinja.JPG|Oi Ebisu Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Haraedo-jinja.JPG|Haraedo Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Shizutama-jinja.JPG|Shizutama Shrine == annual Events == === festivals === The major festivals held at Oi Shrine include:[http://www.ooijinjya.org/events/index.html annual Events] (Official website). * Saitan-sai (January 1) * Setsubun-sai (February 3) * Hina Doll Burning festival (April 7) * Kasuga Shrine festival (June 5) * Misogi no Harae (June 30) * Summer festival, Tanabata festival (August 1) * festival (October 15) * Ebisu Shrine festival, Ebisu Kosha festival (November 19) * Chinka-sai (December 1) === Shimada Grand festival === The shrine's festival is known as the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り). It is said to have started in 1695 (Genroku 8) and is held every three years (years of the Tiger, Snake, Monkey, and Boar) over three days in mid-October. The festival is particularly famous for the daimyo procession on the final day. This procession recreates the grandeur of the Genroku period, with retainers carrying a childbirth prayer sash (the origin of the festival's name) on wooden swords, along with the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊|12=draft}} dance for pestilence avoidance and kabuki dances by children on {{ill|Yatai|ja|屋台|12=draft}} floats. During the festival, Shimada's central area is divided into seven districts, with different events held in each: nagauta (long song) festivals with floats in districts 1-5, Kashima Odori in district 6, and the daimyo procession and parade in district 7. The {{ill|Nenchū Gyōji Jiten|ja|年中行事辞典|12=draft}} lists the [[Yoshida Fire festival|Yoshida Fire festival]] in Yamanashi, the Shimada Grand festival, and the [[Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri|Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri]] in Aichi as the "Three Great Strange festivals of Japan." ‘Second Exhibition: Shimada Grand festival’, Shimada City Museum, 1992, p.6 Additionally, the daimyo procession of the Shimada Grand festival and the Kashima Odori dance are designated as Intangible Folk Cultural Properties by Shizuoka Prefecture. == Cultural Properties == === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** {{ill|Shimada Kashima Odori|ja|島田鹿島踊|12=draft}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on May 13, 1957.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/kashima.html Shimada Kashima Odori] (Shimada City Museum website). ** {{ill|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|ja|島田帯祭の大名行列|12=draft}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on March 12, 1996.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/obimatsuri-daimyou.html Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession] (Shimada City Museum website). == location Information == '''location''' * Oimachi 2316, Shimada, Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan '''Access''' * Train: 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shimada Station|ja|島田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft}} of {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} (JR Tōkai) '''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|ja|島田宿大井川川越遺跡|12=draft}} - National Historic Site. * {{ill|Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|ja|蓬萊橋|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine history * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/885}} '府県郷社明治神社誌料 上'] (National Diet Library Digital Collections) See 885-886 frames. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref=Shimada City History|1978th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=野本寛一|authorlink=野本寛一|year=1987|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=白水社|isbn=4560022208|ref=Oi Shrine (Gods)|1987th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Shimada-juku|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref=Shimada-juku (Heibonsha)|2000th year }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} * [http://www.ooijinjya.org/ Oi Shrine] - Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |location = [[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]] Oimachi 2316 |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|1.18|N|138|10|18.40|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}}
{{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|ハニヤス|12=draft}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} |shrine_rank = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}
{{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} |founded = Unknown |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} |alternate_names = |offices = |annual_festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|12=draft}} |rituals = {{ill|Shimada Grand festival|ja|島田大祭|12=draft}} (Held every three years) }} [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Oi Shrine''' (大井神社, Oi-jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}}, a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}, and currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is dedicated to the spirit of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} and is famous for the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り) held every three years. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history. * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}} (Mizuhanome no Kami) - deity of water. * {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|ハニヤス|12=draft}} (Haniyasu Hime no Kami) - deity of earth. * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} (Amaterasu Sume Ohmikami) - deity of the sun. In historical texts, the shrine is referred to as "Oi no Kami," indicating its origins in the deification of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}}. The three deities mentioned above were enshrined later, particularly Mizuhanome, who reflects the shrine's original connection to water.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} == History == [[File:Oigawa Shimada.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} (Ancient Ritual Site)}}]] [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] == Overview == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Historical records indicate that in 865 (Jōgan 7), the deity of "Oi no Kami" in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国|12=draft}} was elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade. However, the shrine is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} records, making it a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}} (a shrine not listed in the Engishiki). The {{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|12=draft}} records indicate that "Oi Tenjin" had a divine rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} According to tradition, the shrine was originally located in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} in the village of Yahatamura (now part of {{ill|Kawanehon, Shizuoka|ja|川根本町|12=draft}}) but was swept away by a flood in 1276 (Kenji 2) and drifted to Shimada, where it has since been enshrined at its current location.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} A monument now marks the original site of the shrine in the Yahatamura area, although there are several other locations with similar claims.[http://www.ooijinjya.org/knowledge/knowledge03.html Knowledge about the Oi Shrine] (Official website). Due to the frequent flooding of the Oigawa River, over 50 branch shrines of "Oi Shrine" have been established throughout the region.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} During the [[Edo period]], the shrine was temporarily relocated to the Gotemizutani area of Nodamura in 1604 (Keicho 9) due to the collapse of the Oigawa River embankment, but it was returned to its original location in 1615 (Genna 1).{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} In 1689 (Genroku 2), the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the development of {{ill|Shimada-juku|ja|島田宿|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} The shrine received a small donation of land from {{ill|Imagawa Ujizane|ja|今川氏真|12=draft}}, and during the {{ill|Tokugawa|ja|徳川氏|12=draft}} period, it had an exemption of 5 bushels and 9 ho.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1875 and promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1908. Designated on May 13, 1957.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/kashima.html Shimada Kashima Odori] (Shimada City Museum website). ** {{ill|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|ja|島田帯祭の大名行列|12=draft}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on March 12, 1996.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/obimatsuri-daimyou.html Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession] (Shimada City Museum website). == location Information == '''location''' * Oimachi 2316, Shimada, Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan '''Access''' * Train: 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shimada Station|ja|島田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft}} of {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} (JR Tōkai) '''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|ja|島田宿大井川川越遺跡|12=draft}} - National Historic Site. * {{ill|Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|ja|蓬萊橋|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine history * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/885}} '府県郷社明治神社誌料 上'] (National Diet Library Digital Collections) See 885-886 frames. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref=Shimada City History|1978th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=野本寛一|authorlink=野本寛一|year=1987|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=白水社|isbn=4560022208|ref=Oi Shrine (Gods)|1987th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Shimada-juku|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref=Shimada-juku (Heibonsha)|2000th year }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} * [http://www.ooijinjya.org/ Oi Shrine] - Official website * {{Portal Regional Culture Assets|22_shizuoka|7277|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession (video)}} {{Shinto-navbox}} {{Normdaten}} {{Izanami-firebirths}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimada]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shimada]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Oi Shrine]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Oi Shrine]] }} [[en:Oi Shrine]] [[sn:Oi Shrine]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BA%95%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B3%B6%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[sn:i Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Shrine}}

{{other uses}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Oi Shrine |image = [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |location = [[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]] Oimachi 2316 |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|1.18|N|138|10|18.40|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}}
{{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|ハニヤス|12=draft}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} |shrine_rank = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}
{{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} |founded = Unknown |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} |alternate_names = |offices = |annual_festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|12=draft}} |rituals = {{ill|Shimada Grand festival|ja|島田大祭|12=draft}} (Held every three years) }} [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Oi Shrine''' (大井神社, Oi-jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}}, a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}, and currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is dedicated to the spirit of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} and is famous for the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り) held every three years. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history. * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}} (Mizuhanome no Kami) - deity of water. * {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|ハニヤス|12=draft}} (Haniyasu Hime no Kami) - deity of earth. * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} (Amaterasu Sume Ohmikami) - deity of the sun. In historical texts, the shrine is referred to as "Oi no Kami," indicating its origins in the deification of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}}. The three deities mentioned above were enshrined later, particularly Mizuhanome, who reflects the shrine's original connection to water.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} == History == [[File:Oigawa Shimada.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} (Ancient Ritual Site)}}]] [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] == Overview == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Historical records indicate that in 865 (Jōgan 7), the deity of "Oi no Kami" in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国|12=draft}} was elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade. However, the shrine is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} records, making it a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}} (a shrine not listed in the Engishiki). The {{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|12=draft}} records indicate that "Oi Tenjin" had a divine rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} According to tradition, the shrine was originally located in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} in the village of Yahatamura (now part of {{ill|Kawanehon, Shizuoka|ja|川根本町|12=draft}}) but was swept away by a flood in 1276 (Kenji 2) and drifted to Shimada, where it has since been enshrined at its current location.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} A monument now marks the original site of the shrine in the Yahatamura area, although there are several other locations with similar claims.[http://www.ooijinjya.org/knowledge/knowledge03.html Knowledge about the Oi Shrine] (Official website). Due to the frequent flooding of the Oigawa River, over 50 branch shrines of "Oi Shrine" have been established throughout the region.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} During the [[Edo period]], the shrine was temporarily relocated to the Gotemizutani area of Nodamura in 1604 (Keicho 9) due to the collapse of the Oigawa River embankment, but it was returned to its original location in 1615 (Genna 1).{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} In 1689 (Genroku 2), the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the development of {{ill|Shimada-juku|ja|島田宿|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} The shrine received a small donation of land from {{ill|Imagawa Ujizane|ja|今川氏真|12=draft}}, and during the {{ill|Tokugawa|ja|徳川氏|12=draft}} period, it had an exemption of 5 bushels and 9 ho.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1875 and promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1908. In 1966 (Showa 41), it was designated a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. === Divine rankings === * Historical records during the Six National Histories period ** 865 (Jōgan 7) December 21, elevated from Junior Sixth rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}) - Recorded as "Oi no Kami." * Post-Six National Histories ** Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|12=draft}}) - Recorded as "Oi Tenjin." == Sub-Shrines == The shrine grounds contain the following five sub-shrines: * Oi Tenmangu ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} ** festival: October 25 * Kasuga Shrine ** Deities: Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, Futsunushi no Mikoto, and nine others ** festival: Early June * Oi Ebisu Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft}} ** festival: November 19 * Haraedo Shrine ** Deities: Haraedo no Ōkami (four deities) ** festival: May 5 * Shizutama Shrine ** Deities: Over 1950 martyrs from Shimada ** festival: April 15 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Kasuga-jinja & Tenmangu.JPG|Oi Tenmangu (right) and Kasuga Shrine (left) File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Ebisu-jinja.JPG|Oi Ebisu Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Haraedo-jinja.JPG|Haraedo Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Shizutama-jinja.JPG|Shizutama Shrine == annual Events == === festivals === The major festivals held at Oi Shrine include:[http://www.ooijinjya.org/events/index.html annual Events] (Official website). * Saitan-sai (January 1) * Setsubun-sai (February 3) * Hina Doll Burning festival (April 7) * Kasuga Shrine festival (June 5) * Misogi no Harae (June 30) * Summer festival, Tanabata festival (August 1) * festival (October 15) * Ebisu Shrine festival, Ebisu Kosha festival (November 19) * Chinka-sai (December 1) === Shimada Grand festival === The shrine's festival is known as the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り). It is said to have started in 1695 (Genroku 8) and is held every three years (years of the Tiger, Snake, Monkey, and Boar) over three days in mid-October. The festival is particularly famous for the daimyo procession on the final day. This procession recreates the grandeur of the Genroku period, with retainers carrying a childbirth prayer sash (the origin of the festival's name) on wooden swords, along with the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊|12=draft}} dance for pestilence avoidance and kabuki dances by children on {{ill|Yatai|ja|屋台|12=draft}} floats. During the festival, Shimada's central area is divided into seven districts, with different events held in each: nagauta (long song) festivals with floats in districts 1-5, Kashima Odori in district 6, and the daimyo procession and parade in district 7. The {{ill|Nenchū Gyōji Jiten|ja|年中行事辞典|12=draft}} lists the [[Yoshida Fire festival|Yoshida Fire festival]] in Yamanashi, the Shimada Grand festival, and the [[Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri|Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri]] in Aichi as the "Three Great Strange festivals of Japan." ‘Second Exhibition: Shimada Grand festival’, Shimada City Museum, 1992, p.6 Additionally, the daimyo procession of the Shimada Grand festival and the Kashima Odori dance are designated as Intangible Folk Cultural Properties by Shizuoka Prefecture. == Cultural Properties == === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** {{ill|Shimada Kashima Odori|ja|島田鹿島踊|12=draft}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on May 13, 1957.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/kashima.html Shimada Kashima Odori] (Shimada City Museum website). ** {{ill|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|ja|島田帯祭の大名行列|12=draft}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on March 12, 1996.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/obimatsuri-daimyou.html Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession] (Shimada City Museum website). == location Information == '''location''' * Oimachi 2316, Shimada, Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan '''Access''' * Train: 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shimada Station|ja|島田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft}} of {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} (JR Tōkai) '''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|ja|島田宿大井川川越遺跡|12=draft}} - National Historic Site. * {{ill|Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|ja|蓬萊橋|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine history * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/885}} '府県郷社明治神社誌料 上'] (National Diet Library Digital Collections) See 885-886 frames. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref=Shimada City History|1978th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=野本寛一|authorlink=野本寛一|year=1987|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=白水社|isbn=4560022208|ref=Oi Shrine (Gods)|1987th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Shimada-juku|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref=Shimada-juku (Heibonsha)|2000th year }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} * [http://www.ooijinjya.org/ Oi Shrine] - Official website * {{Portal Regional Culture Assets|22_shizuoka|7277|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession (video)}} {{Shinto-navbox}} {{Normdaten}} {{Izanami-firebirths}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimada]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shimada]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Oi Shrine]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Oi Shrine]] }} [[en:Oi Shrine]] [[sn:Oi Shrine]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BA%95%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B3%B6%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[sn:i Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Shrine}}

Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |location = [[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]] Oimachi 2316 |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|1.18|N|138|10|18.40|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}}
{{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|ハニヤス|12=draft}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} |shrine_rank = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}
{{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} |founded = Unknown |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} |alternate_names = |offices = |annual_festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|12=draft}} |rituals = {{ill|Shimada Grand festival|ja|島田大祭|12=draft}} (Held every three years) }} [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Oi Shrine''' (大井神社, Oi-jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}}, a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}, and currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is dedicated to the spirit of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} and is famous for the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り) held every three years. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history. * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}} (Mizuhanome no Kami) - deity of water. * {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|ハニヤス|12=draft}} (Haniyasu Hime no Kami) - deity of earth. * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} (Amaterasu Sume Ohmikami) - deity of the sun. In historical texts, the shrine is referred to as "Oi no Kami," indicating its origins in the deification of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}}. The three deities mentioned above were enshrined later, particularly Mizuhanome, who reflects the shrine's original connection to water.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} == History == [[File:Oigawa Shimada.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} (Ancient Ritual Site)}}]] [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] == Overview == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Historical records indicate that in 865 (Jōgan 7), the deity of "Oi no Kami" in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国|12=draft}} was elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade. However, the shrine is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} records, making it a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}} (a shrine not listed in the Engishiki). The {{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|12=draft}} records indicate that "Oi Tenjin" had a divine rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} According to tradition, the shrine was originally located in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} in the village of Yahatamura (now part of {{ill|Kawanehon, Shizuoka|ja|川根本町|12=draft}}) but was swept away by a flood in 1276 (Kenji 2) and drifted to Shimada, where it has since been enshrined at its current location.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} A monument now marks the original site of the shrine in the Yahatamura area, although there are several other locations with similar claims.[http://www.ooijinjya.org/knowledge/knowledge03.html Knowledge about the Oi Shrine] (Official website). Due to the frequent flooding of the Oigawa River, over 50 branch shrines of "Oi Shrine" have been established throughout the region.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} During the [[Edo period]], the shrine was temporarily relocated to the Gotemizutani area of Nodamura in 1604 (Keicho 9) due to the collapse of the Oigawa River embankment, but it was returned to its original location in 1615 (Genna 1).{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} In 1689 (Genroku 2), the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the development of {{ill|Shimada-juku|ja|島田宿|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} The shrine received a small donation of land from {{ill|Imagawa Ujizane|ja|今川氏真|12=draft}}, and during the {{ill|Tokugawa|ja|徳川氏|12=draft}} period, it had an exemption of 5 bushels and 9 ho.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1875 and promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1908. In 1966 (Showa 41), it was designated a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. === Divine rankings === * Historical records during the Six National Histories period ** 865 (Jōgan 7) December 21, elevated from Junior Sixth rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}) - Recorded as "Oi no Kami." * Post-Six National Histories ** Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|12=draft}}) - Recorded as "Oi Tenjin." == Sub-Shrines == The shrine grounds contain the following five sub-shrines: * Oi Tenmangu ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} ** festival: October 25 * Kasuga Shrine ** Deities: Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, Futsunushi no Mikoto, and nine others ** festival: Early June * Oi Ebisu Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft}} ** festival: November 19 * Haraedo Shrine ** Deities: Haraedo no Ōkami (four deities) ** festival: May 5 * Shizutama Shrine ** Deities: Over 1950 martyrs from Shimada ** festival: April 15 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Kasuga-jinja & Tenmangu.JPG|Oi Tenmangu (right) and Kasuga Shrine (left) File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Ebisu-jinja.JPG|Oi Ebisu Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Haraedo-jinja.JPG|Haraedo Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Shizutama-jinja.JPG|Shizutama Shrine == annual Events == === festivals === The major festivals held at Oi Shrine include:[http://www.ooijinjya.org/events/index.html annual Events] (Official website). * Saitan-sai (January 1) * Setsubun-sai (February 3) * Hina Doll Burning festival (April 7) * Kasuga Shrine festival (June 5) * Misogi no Harae (June 30) * Summer festival, Tanabata festival (August 1) * festival (October 15) * Ebisu Shrine festival, Ebisu Kosha festival (November 19) * Chinka-sai (December 1) === Shimada Grand festival === The shrine's festival is known as the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り). It is said to have started in 1695 (Genroku 8) and is held every three years (years of the Tiger, Snake, Monkey, and Boar) over three days in mid-October. The festival is particularly famous for the daimyo procession on the final day. This procession recreates the grandeur of the Genroku period, with retainers carrying a childbirth prayer sash (the origin of the festival's name) on wooden swords, along with the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊|12=draft}} dance for pestilence avoidance and kabuki dances by children on {{ill|Yatai|ja|屋台|12=draft}} floats. During the festival, Shimada's central area is divided into seven districts, with different events held in each: nagauta (long song) festivals with floats in districts 1-5, Kashima Odori in district 6, and the daimyo procession and parade in district 7. The {{ill|Nenchū Gyōji Jiten|ja|年中行事辞典|12=draft}} lists the [[Yoshida Fire festival|Yoshida Fire festival]] in Yamanashi, the Shimada Grand festival, and the [[Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri|Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri]] in Aichi as the "Three Great Strange festivals of Japan." ‘Second Exhibition: Shimada Grand festival’, Shimada City Museum, 1992, p.6 Additionally, the daimyo procession of the Shimada Grand festival and the Kashima Odori dance are designated as Intangible Folk Cultural Properties by Shizuoka Prefecture. == Cultural Properties == === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** {{ill|Shimada Kashima Odori|ja|島田鹿島踊|12=draft}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on May 13, 1957.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/kashima.html Shimada Kashima Odori] (Shimada City Museum website). ** {{ill|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|ja|島田帯祭の大名行列|12=draft}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on March 12, 1996.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/obimatsuri-daimyou.html Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession] (Shimada City Museum website). == location Information == '''location''' * Oimachi 2316, Shimada, Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan '''Access''' * Train: 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shimada Station|ja|島田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft}} of {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} (JR Tōkai) '''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|ja|島田宿大井川川越遺跡|12=draft}} - National Historic Site. * {{ill|Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|ja|蓬萊橋|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine history * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/885}} '府県郷社明治神社誌料 上'] (National Diet Library Digital Collections) See 885-886 frames. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref=Shimada City History|1978th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=野本寛一|authorlink=野本寛一|year=1987|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=白水社|isbn=4560022208|ref=Oi Shrine (Gods)|1987th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Shimada-juku|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref=Shimada-juku (Heibonsha)|2000th year }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} * [http://www.ooijinjya.org/ Oi Shrine] - Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |location = [[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]] Oimachi 2316 |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|1.18|N|138|10|18.40|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}}
{{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|ハニヤス|12=draft}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} |shrine_rank = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}
{{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} |founded = Unknown |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} |alternate_names = |offices = |annual_festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|12=draft}} |rituals = {{ill|Shimada Grand festival|ja|島田大祭|12=draft}} (Held every three years) }} [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Oi Shrine''' (大井神社, Oi-jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}}, a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}}, and currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is dedicated to the spirit of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} and is famous for the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り) held every three years. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history. * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}} (Mizuhanome no Kami) - deity of water. * {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|ハニヤス|12=draft}} (Haniyasu Hime no Kami) - deity of earth. * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} (Amaterasu Sume Ohmikami) - deity of the sun. In historical texts, the shrine is referred to as "Oi no Kami," indicating its origins in the deification of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}}. The three deities mentioned above were enshrined later, particularly Mizuhanome, who reflects the shrine's original connection to water.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} == History == [[File:Oigawa Shimada.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} (Ancient Ritual Site)}}]] [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] == Overview == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Historical records indicate that in 865 (Jōgan 7), the deity of "Oi no Kami" in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国|12=draft}} was elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade. However, the shrine is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} records, making it a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}} (a shrine not listed in the Engishiki). The {{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|12=draft}} records indicate that "Oi Tenjin" had a divine rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} According to tradition, the shrine was originally located in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft}} in the village of Yahatamura (now part of {{ill|Kawanehon, Shizuoka|ja|川根本町|12=draft}}) but was swept away by a flood in 1276 (Kenji 2) and drifted to Shimada, where it has since been enshrined at its current location.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} A monument now marks the original site of the shrine in the Yahatamura area, although there are several other locations with similar claims.[http://www.ooijinjya.org/knowledge/knowledge03.html Knowledge about the Oi Shrine] (Official website). Due to the frequent flooding of the Oigawa River, over 50 branch shrines of "Oi Shrine" have been established throughout the region.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} During the [[Edo period]], the shrine was temporarily relocated to the Gotemizutani area of Nodamura in 1604 (Keicho 9) due to the collapse of the Oigawa River embankment, but it was returned to its original location in 1615 (Genna 1).{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} In 1689 (Genroku 2), the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the development of {{ill|Shimada-juku|ja|島田宿|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} The shrine received a small donation of land from {{ill|Imagawa Ujizane|ja|今川氏真|12=draft}}, and during the {{ill|Tokugawa|ja|徳川氏|12=draft}} period, it had an exemption of 5 bushels and 9 ho.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1875 and promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1908. Designated on May 13, 1957.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/kashima.html Shimada Kashima Odori] (Shimada City Museum website). ** {{ill|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|ja|島田帯祭の大名行列|12=draft}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on March 12, 1996.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/obimatsuri-daimyou.html Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession] (Shimada City Museum website). == location Information == '''location''' * Oimachi 2316, Shimada, Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan '''Access''' * Train: 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shimada Station|ja|島田駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft}} of {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}} (JR Tōkai) '''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|ja|島田宿大井川川越遺跡|12=draft}} - National Historic Site. * {{ill|Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|ja|蓬萊橋|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine history * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/885}} '府県郷社明治神社誌料 上'] (National Diet Library Digital Collections) See 885-886 frames. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref=Shimada City History|1978th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=野本寛一|authorlink=野本寛一|year=1987|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=白水社|isbn=4560022208|ref=Oi Shrine (Gods)|1987th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Shimada-juku|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref=Shimada-juku (Heibonsha)|2000th year }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} * [http://www.ooijinjya.org/ Oi Shrine] - Official website * {{Portal Regional Culture Assets|22_shizuoka|7277|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession (video)}} {{Shinto-navbox}} {{Normdaten}} {{Izanami-firebirths}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimada]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shimada]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Oi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Oi Shrine]] }} [[en:Oi Shrine]] [[sn:Oi Shrine]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BA%95%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B3%B6%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[sn:i Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{other uses}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Oi Shrine |image = [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |location = [[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]] Oimachi 2316 |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|1.18|N|138|10|18.40|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|ハニヤス|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}} |shrine_rank = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} |founded = Unknown |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} |alternate_names = |offices = |annual_festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|12=draft|12=draft}} |rituals = {{ill|Shimada Grand festival|ja|島田大祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (Held every three years) }} [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Oi Shrine''' (大井神社, Oi-jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft|12=draft}}, a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and currently a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is dedicated to the spirit of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft|12=draft}} and is famous for the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り) held every three years. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history. * {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mizuhanome no Kami) - deity of water. * {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu|ja|ハニヤス|12=draft|12=draft}} (Haniyasu Hime no Kami) - deity of earth. * {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}} (Amaterasu Sume Ohmikami) - deity of the sun. In historical texts, the shrine is referred to as "Oi no Kami," indicating its origins in the deification of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft|12=draft}}. The three deities mentioned above were enshrined later, particularly Mizuhanome, who reflects the shrine's original connection to water.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} == History == [[File:Oigawa Shimada.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ancient Ritual Site)}}]] [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] == Overview == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Historical records indicate that in 865 (Jōgan 7), the deity of "Oi no Kami" in {{ill|Suruga Province|ja|駿河国|12=draft|12=draft}} was elevated to the rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade. However, the shrine is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} records, making it a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft|12=draft}} (a shrine not listed in the Engishiki). The {{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}} records indicate that "Oi Tenjin" had a divine rank of Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} According to tradition, the shrine was originally located in the upper reaches of the {{ill|Oigawa River|ja|大井川|12=draft|12=draft}} in the village of Yahatamura (now part of {{ill|Kawanehon, Shizuoka|ja|川根本町|12=draft|12=draft}}) but was swept away by a flood in 1276 (Kenji 2) and drifted to Shimada, where it has since been enshrined at its current location.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} A monument now marks the original site of the shrine in the Yahatamura area, although there are several other locations with similar claims.[http://www.ooijinjya.org/knowledge/knowledge03.html Knowledge about the Oi Shrine] (Official website). Due to the frequent flooding of the Oigawa River, over 50 branch shrines of "Oi Shrine" have been established throughout the region.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} During the [[Edo period]], the shrine was temporarily relocated to the Gotemizutani area of Nodamura in 1604 (Keicho 9) due to the collapse of the Oigawa River embankment, but it was returned to its original location in 1615 (Genna 1).{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} In 1689 (Genroku 2), the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the development of {{ill|Shimada-juku|ja|島田宿|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} The shrine received a small donation of land from {{ill|Imagawa Ujizane|ja|今川氏真|12=draft|12=draft}}, and during the {{ill|Tokugawa|ja|徳川氏|12=draft|12=draft}} period, it had an exemption of 5 bushels and 9 ho.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1875 and promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1908. In 1966 (Showa 41), it was designated a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. === Divine rankings === * Historical records during the Six National Histories period ** 865 (Jōgan 7) December 21, elevated from Junior Sixth rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}) - Recorded as "Oi no Kami." * Post-Six National Histories ** Junior Fifth rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}) - Recorded as "Oi Tenjin." == Sub-Shrines == The shrine grounds contain the following five sub-shrines: * Oi Tenmangu ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} ** festival: October 25 * Kasuga Shrine ** Deities: Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, Futsunushi no Mikoto, and nine others ** festival: Early June * Oi Ebisu Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}} ** festival: November 19 * Haraedo Shrine ** Deities: Haraedo no Ōkami (four deities) ** festival: May 5 * Shizutama Shrine ** Deities: Over 1950 martyrs from Shimada ** festival: April 15 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Kasuga-jinja & Tenmangu.JPG|Oi Tenmangu (right) and Kasuga Shrine (left) File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Ebisu-jinja.JPG|Oi Ebisu Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Haraedo-jinja.JPG|Haraedo Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Shizutama-jinja.JPG|Shizutama Shrine == annual Events == === festivals === The major festivals held at Oi Shrine include:[http://www.ooijinjya.org/events/index.html annual Events] (Official website). * Saitan-sai (January 1) * Setsubun-sai (February 3) * Hina Doll Burning festival (April 7) * Kasuga Shrine festival (June 5) * Misogi no Harae (June 30) * Summer festival, Tanabata festival (August 1) * festival (October 15) * Ebisu Shrine festival, Ebisu Kosha festival (November 19) * Chinka-sai (December 1) === Shimada Grand festival === The shrine's festival is known as the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り). It is said to have started in 1695 (Genroku 8) and is held every three years (years of the Tiger, Snake, Monkey, and Boar) over three days in mid-October. The festival is particularly famous for the daimyo procession on the final day. This procession recreates the grandeur of the Genroku period, with retainers carrying a childbirth prayer sash (the origin of the festival's name) on wooden swords, along with the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊|12=draft|12=draft}} dance for pestilence avoidance and kabuki dances by children on {{ill|Yatai|ja|屋台|12=draft|12=draft}} floats. During the festival, Shimada's central area is divided into seven districts, with different events held in each: nagauta (long song) festivals with floats in districts 1-5, Kashima Odori in district 6, and the daimyo procession and parade in district 7. The {{ill|Nenchū Gyōji Jiten|ja|年中行事辞典|12=draft|12=draft}} lists the [[Yoshida Fire festival|Yoshida Fire festival]] in Yamanashi, the Shimada Grand festival, and the [[Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri|Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri]] in Aichi as the "Three Great Strange festivals of Japan." ‘Second Exhibition: Shimada Grand festival’, Shimada City Museum, 1992, p.6 Additionally, the daimyo procession of the Shimada Grand festival and the Kashima Odori dance are designated as Intangible Folk Cultural Properties by Shizuoka Prefecture. == Cultural Properties == === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** {{ill|Shimada Kashima Odori|ja|島田鹿島踊|12=draft|12=draft}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on May 13, 1957.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/kashima.html Shimada Kashima Odori] (Shimada City Museum website). ** {{ill|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|ja|島田帯祭の大名行列|12=draft|12=draft}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on March 12, 1996.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/obimatsuri-daimyou.html Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession] (Shimada City Museum website). == location Information == '''location''' * Oimachi 2316, Shimada, Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan '''Access''' * Train: 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shimada Station|ja|島田駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Tōkai) '''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|ja|島田宿大井川川越遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} - National Historic Site. * {{ill|Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|ja|蓬萊橋|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine history * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/885}} '府県郷社明治神社誌料 上'] (National Diet Library Digital Collections) See 885-886 frames. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref=Shimada City History|1978th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=野本寛一|authorlink=野本寛一|year=1987|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=白水社|isbn=4560022208|ref=Oi Shrine (Gods)|1987th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Shimada-juku|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref=Shimada-juku (Heibonsha)|2000th year }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} * [http://www.ooijinjya.org/ Oi Shrine] - Official website * {{Portal Regional Culture Assets|22_shizuoka|7277|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession (video)}} {{Shinto-navbox}} {{Normdaten}} {{Izanami-firebirths}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimada]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shimada]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Oi Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oi Shrine]] [[en:Oi Shrine]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BA%95%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B3%B6%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[sn:i Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oi Shrine}}
