{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432211]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{other uses}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Oi Shrine |image = [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[:en:Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]] Oimachi 2316 |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|1.18|N|138|10|18.40|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]]
[[:en:Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|Haniyasu]]
[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] |founded = Unknown |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternate_names = |offices = |annual_festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |rituals = {{ill|Shimada Grand festival|ja|島田大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Grand festival|qq=|1=Shimada Taisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48764792}} (Held every three years) }} [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Oi Shrine''' (大井神社, Oi-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is dedicated to the spirit of the [[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]] and is famous for the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り) held every three years. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Mizuhanome no Kami) - deity of water. * [[:en:Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|Haniyasu]] (Haniyasu Hime no Kami) - deity of earth. * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (Amaterasu Sume Ohmikami) - deity of the sun. In historical texts, the shrine is referred to as "Oi no Kami," indicating its origins in the deification of the [[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]]. The three deities mentioned above were enshrined later, particularly Mizuhanome, who reflects the shrine's original connection to water.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} == History == [[File:Oigawa Shimada.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]] (Ancient Ritual Site)}}]] [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] == Overview == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Historical records indicate that in 865 (Jōgan 7), the deity of "Oi no Kami" in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] was elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. However, the shrine is not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records, making it a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (a shrine not listed in the Engishiki). The {{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga no Kuni Shingi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} records indicate that "Oi Tenjin" had a divine Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} According to tradition, the shrine was originally located in the upper reaches of the [[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]] in the village of Yahatamura (now part of [[:en:Kawanehon|Kawanehon, Shizuoka]]) but was swept away by a flood in 1276 (Kenji 2) and drifted to Shimada, where it has since been enshrined at its current location.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} A monument now marks the original site of the shrine in the Yahatamura area, although there are several other locations with similar claims.[http://www.ooijinjya.org/knowledge/knowledge03.html Knowledge about the Oi Shrine] (Official website). Due to the frequent flooding of the Oigawa River, over 50 branch shrines of "Oi Shrine" have been established throughout the region.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was temporarily relocated to the Gotemizutani area of Nodamura in 1604 (Keicho 9) due to the collapse of the Oigawa River embankment, but it was returned to its original location in 1615 (Genna 1).{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} In 1689 (Genroku 2), the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the development of [[:en:Shimada-juku|Shimada-juku]].{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} The shrine received a small donation of land from [[:en:Imagawa Ujizane|Imagawa Ujizane]], and during the [[:en:Tokugawa clan|Tokugawa]] period, it had an exemption of 5 bushels and 9 ho.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1875 and promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1908. In 1966 (Showa 41), it was designated a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. === Divine Rankings === * Historical records during the Six National Histories period ** 865 (Jōgan 7) December 21, elevated from Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) - Recorded as "Oi no Kami." * Post-Six National Histories ** Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga no Kuni Shingi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) - Recorded as "Oi Tenjin." == Sub-Shrines == The shrine grounds contain the following five sub-shrines: * Oi Tenmangu ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} ** festival: October 25 * Kasuga Shrine ** Deities: Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, Futsunushi no Mikoto, and nine others ** festival: Early June * Oi Ebisu Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} ** festival: November 19 * Haraedo Shrine ** Deities: Haraedo no Ōkami (four deities) ** festival: May 5 * Shizutama Shrine ** Deities: Over 1950 martyrs from Shimada ** festival: April 15 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Kasuga-jinja & Tenmangu.JPG|Oi Tenmangu (right) and Kasuga Shrine (left) File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Ebisu-jinja.JPG|Oi Ebisu Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Haraedo-jinja.JPG|Haraedo Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Shizutama-jinja.JPG|Shizutama Shrine == annual Events == === festivals === The major festivals held at Oi Shrine include:[http://www.ooijinjya.org/events/index.html annual Events] (Official website). * Saitan-sai (January 1) * Setsubun-sai (February 3) * Hina Doll Burning festival (April 7) * Kasuga Shrine festival (June 5) * Misogi no Harae (June 30) * Summer festival, Tanabata festival (August 1) * festival (October 15) * Ebisu Shrine festival, Ebisu Kosha festival (November 19) * Chinka-sai (December 1) === Shimada Grand festival === The shrine's festival is known as the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り). It is said to have started in 1695 (Genroku 8) and is held every three years (years of the Tiger, Snake, Monkey, and Boar) over three days in mid-October. The festival is particularly famous for the daimyo procession on the final day. This procession recreates the grandeur of the Genroku period, with retainers carrying a childbirth prayer sash (the origin of the festival's name) on wooden swords, along with the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿島踊り|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}} dance for pestilence avoidance and kabuki dances by children on [[:en:Market stall|Yatai]] floats. During the festival, Shimada's central area is divided into seven districts, with different events held in each: nagauta (long song) festivals with floats in districts 1-5, Kashima Odori in district 6, and the daimyo procession and parade in district 7. The {{ill|Nenchū Gyōji Jiten|ja|年中行事辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenchū Gyōji Jiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lists the [[Yoshida Fire Festival|Yoshida Fire festival]] in Yamanashi, the Shimada Grand festival, and the [[Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri|Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri]] in Aichi as the "Three Great Strange festivals of Japan." ‘Second Exhibition: Shimada Grand festival’, Shimada City Museum, 1992, p.6 Additionally, the daimyo procession of the Shimada Grand festival and the Kashima Odori dance are designated as Intangible Folk Cultural Properties by Shizuoka Prefecture. == Cultural Properties == === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** {{ill|Shimada Kashima Odori|ja|島田鹿島踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Kashima Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on May 13, 1957.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/kashima.html Shimada Kashima Odori] (Shimada City Museum website). ** {{ill|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|ja|島田帯祭の大名行列|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on March 12, 1996.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/obimatsuri-daimyou.html Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession] (Shimada City Museum website). == location Information == '''location''' * Oimachi 2316, Shimada, Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan '''Access''' * Train: 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shimada Station|ja|島田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Station|qq=|1=Shimada Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052398}} on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] of [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|Central Japan Railway Company]] (JR Tōkai) '''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|ja|島田宿大井川川越遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|qq=|1=Shimada-shuku Ōi-gawa Kawagoshi Rerics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48442779}} - National Historic Site. * {{ill|Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|ja|蓬萊橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|qq=|1=Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621618}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine history * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/885}} '府県郷社明治神社誌料 上'] (National Diet Library Digital Collections) See 885-886 frames. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref=Shimada City History|1978th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=野本寛一|authorlink=野本寛一|year=1987|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=白水社|isbn=4560022208|ref=Oi Shrine (Gods)|1987th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Shimada-juku|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref=Shimada-juku (Heibonsha)|2000th year }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} * [http://www.ooijinjya.org/ Oi Shrine] - Official website * {{Portal Regional Culture Assets|22_shizuoka|7277|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession (video)}} {{Shinto-navbox}} {{Izanami-firebirths}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (島田市)|version=101896604|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimada]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Oi Shrine]] [[sn:E5%A4%A7%E4%BA%95%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B3%B6%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[sn:i Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimada]] [[Category:History of Shimada]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432211]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{other uses}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Oi Shrine |image = [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[:en:Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]] Oimachi 2316 |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|1.18|N|138|10|18.40|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]]
[[:en:Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|Haniyasu]]
[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] |founded = Unknown |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternate_names = |offices = |annual_festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |rituals = {{ill|Shimada Grand festival|ja|島田大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Grand festival|qq=|1=Shimada Taisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48764792}} (Held every three years) }} [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Oi Shrine''' (大井神社, Oi-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is dedicated to the spirit of the [[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]] and is famous for the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り) held every three years. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Mizuhanome no Kami) - deity of water. * [[:en:Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|Haniyasu]] (Haniyasu Hime no Kami) - deity of earth. * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (Amaterasu Sume Ohmikami) - deity of the sun. In historical texts, the shrine is referred to as "Oi no Kami," indicating its origins in the deification of the [[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]]. The three deities mentioned above were enshrined later, particularly Mizuhanome, who reflects the shrine's original connection to water.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} == History == [[File:Oigawa Shimada.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]] (Ancient Ritual Site)}}]] [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] == Overview == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Historical records indicate that in 865 (Jōgan 7), the deity of "Oi no Kami" in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] was elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. However, the shrine is not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records, making it a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (a shrine not listed in the Engishiki). The {{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga no Kuni Shingi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} records indicate that "Oi Tenjin" had a divine Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} According to tradition, the shrine was originally located in the upper reaches of the [[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]] in the village of Yahatamura (now part of [[:en:Kawanehon|Kawanehon, Shizuoka]]) but was swept away by a flood in 1276 (Kenji 2) and drifted to Shimada, where it has since been enshrined at its current location.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} A monument now marks the original site of the shrine in the Yahatamura area, although there are several other locations with similar claims.[http://www.ooijinjya.org/knowledge/knowledge03.html Knowledge about the Oi Shrine] (Official website). Due to the frequent flooding of the Oigawa River, over 50 branch shrines of "Oi Shrine" have been established throughout the region.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was temporarily relocated to the Gotemizutani area of Nodamura in 1604 (Keicho 9) due to the collapse of the Oigawa River embankment, but it was returned to its original location in 1615 (Genna 1).{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} In 1689 (Genroku 2), the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the development of [[:en:Shimada-juku|Shimada-juku]].{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} The shrine received a small donation of land from [[:en:Imagawa Ujizane|Imagawa Ujizane]], and during the [[:en:Tokugawa clan|Tokugawa]] period, it had an exemption of 5 bushels and 9 ho.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1875 and promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1908. In 1966 (Showa 41), it was designated a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. === Divine Rankings === * Historical records during the Six National Histories period ** 865 (Jōgan 7) December 21, elevated from Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) - Recorded as "Oi no Kami." * Post-Six National Histories ** Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga no Kuni Shingi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) - Recorded as "Oi Tenjin." == Sub-Shrines == The shrine grounds contain the following five sub-shrines: * Oi Tenmangu ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} ** festival: October 25 * Kasuga Shrine ** Deities: Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, Futsunushi no Mikoto, and nine others ** festival: Early June * Oi Ebisu Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} ** festival: November 19 * Haraedo Shrine ** Deities: Haraedo no Ōkami (four deities) ** festival: May 5 * Shizutama Shrine ** Deities: Over 1950 martyrs from Shimada ** festival: April 15 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Kasuga-jinja & Tenmangu.JPG|Oi Tenmangu (right) and Kasuga Shrine (left) File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Ebisu-jinja.JPG|Oi Ebisu Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Haraedo-jinja.JPG|Haraedo Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Shizutama-jinja.JPG|Shizutama Shrine == annual Events == === festivals === The major festivals held at Oi Shrine include:[http://www.ooijinjya.org/events/index.html annual Events] (Official website). * Saitan-sai (January 1) * Setsubun-sai (February 3) * Hina Doll Burning festival (April 7) * Kasuga Shrine festival (June 5) * Misogi no Harae (June 30) * Summer festival, Tanabata festival (August 1) * festival (October 15) * Ebisu Shrine festival, Ebisu Kosha festival (November 19) * Chinka-sai (December 1) === Shimada Grand festival === The shrine's festival is known as the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り). It is said to have started in 1695 (Genroku 8) and is held every three years (years of the Tiger, Snake, Monkey, and Boar) over three days in mid-October. The festival is particularly famous for the daimyo procession on the final day. This procession recreates the grandeur of the Genroku period, with retainers carrying a childbirth prayer sash (the origin of the festival's name) on wooden swords, along with the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿島踊り|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}} dance for pestilence avoidance and kabuki dances by children on [[:en:Market stall|Yatai]] floats. During the festival, Shimada's central area is divided into seven districts, with different events held in each: nagauta (long song) festivals with floats in districts 1-5, Kashima Odori in district 6, and the daimyo procession and parade in district 7. The {{ill|Nenchū Gyōji Jiten|ja|年中行事辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenchū Gyōji Jiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lists the [[Yoshida Fire Festival|Yoshida Fire festival]] in Yamanashi, the Shimada Grand festival, and the [[Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri|Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri]] in Aichi as the "Three Great Strange festivals of Japan." ‘Second Exhibition: Shimada Grand festival’, Shimada City Museum, 1992, p.6 Additionally, the daimyo procession of the Shimada Grand festival and the Kashima Odori dance are designated as Intangible Folk Cultural Properties by Shizuoka Prefecture. == Cultural Properties == === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** {{ill|Shimada Kashima Odori|ja|島田鹿島踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Kashima Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on May 13, 1957.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/kashima.html Shimada Kashima Odori] (Shimada City Museum website). ** {{ill|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|ja|島田帯祭の大名行列|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on March 12, 1996.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/obimatsuri-daimyou.html Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession] (Shimada City Museum website). == location Information == '''location''' * Oimachi 2316, Shimada, Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan '''Access''' * Train: 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shimada Station|ja|島田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Station|qq=|1=Shimada Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052398}} on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] of [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|Central Japan Railway Company]] (JR Tōkai) '''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|ja|島田宿大井川川越遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|qq=|1=Shimada-shuku Ōi-gawa Kawagoshi Rerics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48442779}} - National Historic Site. * {{ill|Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|ja|蓬萊橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|qq=|1=Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621618}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine history * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/885}} '府県郷社明治神社誌料 上'] (National Diet Library Digital Collections) See 885-886 frames. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref=Shimada City History|1978th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=野本寛一|authorlink=野本寛一|year=1987|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=白水社|isbn=4560022208|ref=Oi Shrine (Gods)|1987th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Shimada-juku|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref=Shimada-juku (Heibonsha)|2000th year }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} * [http://www.ooijinjya.org/ Oi Shrine] - Official website * {{Portal Regional Culture Assets|22_shizuoka|7277|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession (video)}} {{Shinto-navbox}} {{Izanami-firebirths}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大井神社 (島田市)|version=101896604|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimada]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Oi Shrine]] [[sn:E5%A4%A7%E4%BA%95%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B3%B6%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:大井神社]] [[sn:i Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimada]] [[Category:History of Shimada]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432211]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:27, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{other uses}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Oi Shrine |image = [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[:en:Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]] Oimachi 2316 |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|1.18|N|138|10|18.40|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]]
[[:en:Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|Haniyasu]]
[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] |founded = Unknown |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternate_names = |offices = |annual_festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |rituals = {{ill|Shimada Grand festival|ja|島田大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Grand festival|qq=|1=Shimada Taisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48764792}} (Held every three years) }} [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Oi Shrine''' (大井神社, Oi-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is dedicated to the spirit of the [[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]] and is famous for the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り) held every three years. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Mizuhanome no Kami) - deity of water. * [[:en:Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|Haniyasu]] (Haniyasu Hime no Kami) - deity of earth. * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (Amaterasu Sume Ohmikami) - deity of the sun. In historical texts, the shrine is referred to as "Oi no Kami," indicating its origins in the deification of the [[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]]. The three deities mentioned above were enshrined later, particularly Mizuhanome, who reflects the shrine's original connection to water.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} == History == [[File:Oigawa Shimada.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]] (Ancient Ritual Site)}}]] [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] == Overview == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Historical records indicate that in 865 (Jōgan 7), the deity of "Oi no Kami" in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] was elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. However, the shrine is not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records, making it a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (a shrine not listed in the Engishiki). The {{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga no Kuni Shingi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} records indicate that "Oi Tenjin" had a divine Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} According to tradition, the shrine was originally located in the upper reaches of the [[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]] in the village of Yahatamura (now part of [[:en:Kawanehon|Kawanehon, Shizuoka]]) but was swept away by a flood in 1276 (Kenji 2) and drifted to Shimada, where it has since been enshrined at its current location.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} A monument now marks the original site of the shrine in the Yahatamura area, although there are several other locations with similar claims.[http://www.ooijinjya.org/knowledge/knowledge03.html Knowledge about the Oi Shrine] (Official website). Due to the frequent flooding of the Oigawa River, over 50 branch shrines of "Oi Shrine" have been established throughout the region.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was temporarily relocated to the Gotemizutani area of Nodamura in 1604 (Keicho 9) due to the collapse of the Oigawa River embankment, but it was returned to its original location in 1615 (Genna 1).{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} In 1689 (Genroku 2), the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the development of [[:en:Shimada-juku|Shimada-juku]].{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} The shrine received a small donation of land from [[:en:Imagawa Ujizane|Imagawa Ujizane]], and during the [[:en:Tokugawa clan|Tokugawa]] period, it had an exemption of 5 bushels and 9 ho.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1875 and promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1908. In 1966 (Showa 41), it was designated a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. === Divine Rankings === * Historical records during the Six National Histories period ** 865 (Jōgan 7) December 21, elevated from Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) - Recorded as "Oi no Kami." * Post-Six National Histories ** Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga no Kuni Shingi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) - Recorded as "Oi Tenjin." == Sub-Shrines == The shrine grounds contain the following five sub-shrines: * Oi Tenmangu ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} ** festival: October 25 * Kasuga Shrine ** Deities: Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, Futsunushi no Mikoto, and nine others ** festival: Early June * Oi Ebisu Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} ** festival: November 19 * Haraedo Shrine ** Deities: Haraedo no Ōkami (four deities) ** festival: May 5 * Shizutama Shrine ** Deities: Over 1950 martyrs from Shimada ** festival: April 15 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Kasuga-jinja & Tenmangu.JPG|Oi Tenmangu (right) and Kasuga Shrine (left) File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Ebisu-jinja.JPG|Oi Ebisu Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Haraedo-jinja.JPG|Haraedo Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Shizutama-jinja.JPG|Shizutama Shrine == annual Events == === festivals === The major festivals held at Oi Shrine include:[http://www.ooijinjya.org/events/index.html annual Events] (Official website). * Saitan-sai (January 1) * Setsubun-sai (February 3) * Hina Doll Burning festival (April 7) * Kasuga Shrine festival (June 5) * Misogi no Harae (June 30) * Summer festival, Tanabata festival (August 1) * festival (October 15) * Ebisu Shrine festival, Ebisu Kosha festival (November 19) * Chinka-sai (December 1) === Shimada Grand festival === The shrine's festival is known as the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り). It is said to have started in 1695 (Genroku 8) and is held every three years (years of the Tiger, Snake, Monkey, and Boar) over three days in mid-October. The festival is particularly famous for the daimyo procession on the final day. This procession recreates the grandeur of the Genroku period, with retainers carrying a childbirth prayer sash (the origin of the festival's name) on wooden swords, along with the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿島踊り|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}} dance for pestilence avoidance and kabuki dances by children on [[:en:Market stall|Yatai]] floats. During the festival, Shimada's central area is divided into seven districts, with different events held in each: nagauta (long song) festivals with floats in districts 1-5, Kashima Odori in district 6, and the daimyo procession and parade in district 7. The {{ill|Nenchū Gyōji Jiten|ja|年中行事辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenchū Gyōji Jiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lists the [[Yoshida Fire Festival|Yoshida Fire festival]] in Yamanashi, the Shimada Grand festival, and the [[Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri|Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri]] in Aichi as the "Three Great Strange festivals of Japan." ‘Second Exhibition: Shimada Grand festival’, Shimada City Museum, 1992, p.6 Additionally, the daimyo procession of the Shimada Grand festival and the Kashima Odori dance are designated as Intangible Folk Cultural Properties by Shizuoka Prefecture. == Cultural Properties == === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** {{ill|Shimada Kashima Odori|ja|島田鹿島踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Kashima Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on May 13, 1957.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/kashima.html Shimada Kashima Odori] (Shimada City Museum website). ** {{ill|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|ja|島田帯祭の大名行列|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on March 12, 1996.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/obimatsuri-daimyou.html Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession] (Shimada City Museum website). == location Information == '''location''' * Oimachi 2316, Shimada, Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan '''Access''' * Train: 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shimada Station|ja|島田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Station|qq=|1=Shimada Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052398}} on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] of [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|Central Japan Railway Company]] (JR Tōkai) '''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|ja|島田宿大井川川越遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|qq=|1=Shimada-shuku Ōi-gawa Kawagoshi Rerics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48442779}} - National Historic Site. * {{ill|Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|ja|蓬萊橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|qq=|1=Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621618}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine history * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/885}} '府県郷社明治神社誌料 上'] (National Diet Library Digital Collections) See 885-886 frames. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref=Shimada City History|1978th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=野本寛一|authorlink=野本寛一|year=1987|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=白水社|isbn=4560022208|ref=Oi Shrine (Gods)|1987th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Shimada-juku|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref=Shimada-juku (Heibonsha)|2000th year }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} * [http://www.ooijinjya.org/ Oi Shrine] - Official website * {{Portal Regional Culture Assets|22_shizuoka|7277|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession (video)}} {{Shinto-navbox}} {{Izanami-firebirths}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimada]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Oi Shrine]] [[sn:E5%A4%A7%E4%BA%95%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E5%B3%B6%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[sn:i Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Shimada]] [[Category:History of Shimada]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} [[ja:大井神社 (島田市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大井神社 |画像 = [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) haiden.JPG|270px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[島田市]]大井町2316 |位置 = {{coord|34|50|1.18|N|138|10|18.40|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[ミヅハノメ|弥都波能売神]]
[[ハニヤス|波邇夜須比売神]]
[[天照大神|天照皇大神]] |神体 = |社格 = [[国史見在社]]
旧[[県社]]
[[別表神社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月15日]] |神事 = [[#島田大祭|帯祭り]]（3年に1度） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=大井神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) torii.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|正面鳥居}}]] '''大井神社'''（おおいじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[島田市]]大井町にある[[神社]]。[[国史見在社]]、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 [[大井川]]の神霊を祀る神社で、3年に1度行う[[島田大祭]]（帯祭り）で知られる。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱神社由緒書。。 * [[ミヅハノメ|弥都波能売神]] （みづはのめのかみ） - 水の神。 * [[ハニヤス|波邇夜須比売神]] （はにやすひめのかみ） - 土の神。 * [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] （あまてらすすめおおみかみ） - 日の神。 文献上で「大井神」と見られるように、[[大井川]]の神格化が始まりとされる{{Sfn|島田市史 上|1978年|pp=159-161}}。上記の人格神3神はいずれも後世に祭神とされたものであるが、特に水神である弥都波能売神に大井川の神格化の名残りが指摘される{{Sfn|島田宿（平凡社）|2000年}}。 == 歴史 == [[File:Oigawa Shimada.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[大井川]]（古代の祭祀対象）}}]] [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) honden.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|本殿}}]] === 概史 === 創建は不詳。[[六国史|国史]]では、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]7年（865年）に[[駿河国]]の「大井神」の[[神階]]が従五位下に昇叙された旨の記事がある。しかし『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]には記載がないため、いわゆる[[国史見在社]]にあたる（神名帳では志太郡条が欠落する{{Sfn|島田市史 上|1978年|pp=159-161}}）。また『駿河国内神名帳』では、[[志太郡]]に「大井天神」の神名で正五位下の神階を有する旨とともに記載されている{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 大井神社では流着伝説が残されており、元は大井川上流の谷畠村の大沢（現・[[榛原郡]][[川根本町]]）に祀られていたが、[[建治]]2年（1276年）の洪水で流されて島田に漂着し、以後は島田の下島（現在の御旅所の地）に祀られるようになったという{{Sfn|島田宿（平凡社）|2000年}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。この元宮伝承地である大沢地区では、現在までに「大井神社旧社跡」碑が建てられている（ただし元宮伝承地は大沢地区の他にも数ヶ所ある）[http://www.ooijinjya.org/knowledge/knowledge03.html まめ知識 > 大井の神様は上流から大洪水に乗っておとずれる]（公式サイト）。。大井川は農耕に欠かせない一方で洪水も繰り返したことから、流域では大井川に対する信仰が深く、「大井神社」が50社以上も分布することが知られる{{Sfn|島田市史 上|1978年|pp=159-161}}。当社はそれらの中で中心的な存在になる{{Sfn|島田市史 上|1978年|pp=159-161}}。 [[江戸時代]]、[[慶長]]9年（1604年）には大井川の堤防決壊により野田村の御手水ヶ谷に一時遷座したが、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（1615年）に下島に戻った{{Sfn|島田宿（平凡社）|2000年}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。さらに[[元禄]]2年（1689年）には、[[島田宿]]の発展に伴い現在地に遷座している{{Sfn|島田宿（平凡社）|2000年}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 社領としては、[[今川氏真]]から若干の朱印地が寄せられたほか、[[徳川氏]]の時に除地5畝9歩があったという{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]8年（1875年）に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列し、明治41年（1908年）に[[県社]]に昇格した{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。戦後、[[昭和]]41年（1966年）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加えられた。 === 神階 === * 六国史時代における神階奉叙の記録 ** [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]7年（[[865年]]）12月21日、正六位上から従五位下 （『[[日本三代実録]]』） - 表記は「大井神」。 * 六国史以後 ** 正五位下 （『駿河国内神名帳』） - 表記は「大井天神」。 == 摂末社 == 境内には次の5社が鎮座する。 * 大井天満宮 ** 祭神：[[菅原道真]] ** 例祭：10月25日 * 春日神社 ** 祭神：健御賀豆智命、伊波比主命ほか9柱 ** 例祭：6月上旬 * 大井恵比寿神社 ** 祭神：大国主命、事代主命 ** 例祭：11月19日 * 祓戸神社 ** 祭神：祓戸四神 ** 例祭：5月5日 * 静霊神社 ** 祭神：島田市出身の国事殉難者1950余柱 ** 例祭：4月15日 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Kasuga-jinja & Tenmangu.JPG|大井天満宮（右）と春日神社（左） File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Ebisu-jinja.JPG|大井恵比寿神社 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Haraedo-jinja.JPG|祓戸神社 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Shizutama-jinja.JPG|静霊神社 == 祭事 == === 年間祭事 === 大井神社で年間に行われる主な祭事の一覧[http://www.ooijinjya.org/events/index.html 年中行事]（公式サイト）。。 * 歳旦祭 （1月1日） * 節分祭 （2月3日） * 雛人形焼納祭 （4月7日） * 春日神社例祭 （6月5日） * みそぎの祓い （6月30日） * 夏祭り、七夕祭り （8月1日） * '''例祭''' （10月15日） * 恵比寿神社例祭、恵比寿講社祭 （11月19日） * 鎮火祭 （12月1日） === 島田大祭 === 大井神社の祭は[[島田大祭]]（帯まつり）と称される。[[元禄]]8年（1695年）に始まると伝えられ、現在は3年に1度（寅年・巳年・申年・亥年）の10月中旬の3日間に行われる。 帯祭りでは、特に最終日の神輿渡御神事の際の大名行列が知られる。この行列は元禄絵巻さながらのもので、大奴が安産祈願の帯を木太刀に下げて練り歩く（「帯祭り」の由来）ほか、疫病退散を願う[[鹿島踊り]]、子供が[[長唄]]に合わせて[[歌舞伎]]踊りを披露する[[山車|屋台]]等が付く。祭りの間、島田市の中心部は7つの街（がい）に分けられ、1-5街では屋台を中心とした長唄祭り、6街では鹿島踊り、7街では大名行列と大奴の練り歩きを行う。 『年中行事辞典』は[[山梨県]][[富士吉田市]]の[[吉田の火祭]]、島田大祭、[[愛知県]][[稲沢市]]の[[国府宮はだか祭]]を「日本三奇祭」としている『第二回企画展 島田大祭展』島田市博物館、1992年、p.6。また大名行列「島田帯祭の大名行列」として、鹿島踊りは「島田鹿島踊」としてそれぞれ静岡県指定無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 == 文化財 == === 静岡県指定文化財 === * 無形民俗文化財 ** 島田鹿島踊 - 大井神社ほか。1957年（昭和32年）5月13日指定[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/kashima.html 島田鹿島踊]（島田市博物館ホームページ）。。 ** 島田帯祭の大名行列 - 大井神社ほか。1996年（平成8年）3月12日指定[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/obimatsuri-daimyou.html 島田帯祭の大名行列]（島田市博物館ホームページ）。。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[静岡県]][[島田市]]大井町2316 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：[[東海旅客鉄道]]（JR東海）[[東海道本線]] [[島田駅 (静岡県)|島田駅]] （徒歩約5分） '''周辺''' * [[島田宿|島田宿大井川川越遺跡]] - 国の史跡。 * [[蓬萊橋 (大井川)|蓬萊橋]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=大井神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/885}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）885-886コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|島田市史 上|1978年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=野本寛一|authorlink=野本寛一|year=1987|chapter=大井神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=[[白水社]]|isbn=4560022208|ref={{Harvid|大井神社（神々）|1987年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=島田宿|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|島田宿（平凡社）|2000年}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} * [http://www.ooijinjya.org/ 大井神社] - 公式サイト * {{地域文化資産ポータル|22_shizuoka|7277|島田帯祭の大名行列（動画）}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおいしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:島田市の歴史]] [[Category:島田市の建築物]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]]

水の神。 * [[ハニヤス|波邇夜須比売神]] （はにやすひめのかみ） - 土の神。 * [[天照大神|天照皇大神]] （あまてらすすめおおみかみ） - 日の神。 文献上で「大井神」と見られるように、[[大井川]]の神格化が始まりとされる{{Sfn|島田市史 上|1978年|pp=159-161}}。上記の人格神3神はいずれも後世に祭神とされたものであるが、特に水神である弥都波能売神に大井川の神格化の名残りが指摘される{{Sfn|島田宿（平凡社）|2000年}}。 == 歴史 == [[File:Oigawa Shimada.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|[[大井川]]（古代の祭祀対象）}}]] [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) honden.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|本殿}}]] === 概史 === 創建は不詳。[[六国史|国史]]では、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]7年（865年）に[[駿河国]]の「大井神」の[[神階]]が従五位下に昇叙された旨の記事がある。しかし『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]には記載がないため、いわゆる[[国史見在社]]にあたる（神名帳では志太郡条が欠落する{{Sfn|島田市史 上|1978年|pp=159-161}}）。また『駿河国内神名帳』では、[[志太郡]]に「大井天神」の神名で正五位下の神階を有する旨とともに記載されている{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 大井神社では流着伝説が残されており、元は大井川上流の谷畠村の大沢（現・[[榛原郡]][[川根本町]]）に祀られていたが、[[建治]]2年（1276年）の洪水で流されて島田に漂着し、以後は島田の下島（現在の御旅所の地）に祀られるようになったという{{Sfn|島田宿（平凡社）|2000年}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。この元宮伝承地である大沢地区では、現在までに「大井神社旧社跡」碑が建てられている（ただし元宮伝承地は大沢地区の他にも数ヶ所ある）[http://www.ooijinjya.org/knowledge/knowledge03.html まめ知識 > 大井の神様は上流から大洪水に乗っておとずれる]（公式サイト）。。大井川は農耕に欠かせない一方で洪水も繰り返したことから、流域では大井川に対する信仰が深く、「大井神社」が50社以上も分布することが知られる{{Sfn|島田市史 上|1978年|pp=159-161}}。当社はそれらの中で中心的な存在になる{{Sfn|島田市史 上|1978年|pp=159-161}}。 [[江戸時代]]、[[慶長]]9年（1604年）には大井川の堤防決壊により野田村の御手水ヶ谷に一時遷座したが、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]元年（1615年）に下島に戻った{{Sfn|島田宿（平凡社）|2000年}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。さらに[[元禄]]2年（1689年）には、[[島田宿]]の発展に伴い現在地に遷座している{{Sfn|島田宿（平凡社）|2000年}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 社領としては、[[今川氏真]]から若干の朱印地が寄せられたほか、[[徳川氏]]の時に除地5畝9歩があったという{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]8年（1875年）に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列し、明治41年（1908年）に[[県社]]に昇格した{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。戦後、[[昭和]]41年（1966年）に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加えられた。 === 神階 === * 六国史時代における神階奉叙の記録 ** [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]7年（[[865年]]）12月21日、正六位上から従五位下 （『[[日本三代実録]]』） - 表記は「大井神」。 * 六国史以後 ** 正五位下 （『駿河国内神名帳』） - 表記は「大井天神」。 == 摂末社 == 境内には次の5社が鎮座する。 * 大井天満宮 ** 祭神：[[菅原道真]] ** 例祭：10月25日 * 春日神社 ** 祭神：健御賀豆智命、伊波比主命ほか9柱 ** 例祭：6月上旬 * 大井恵比寿神社 ** 祭神：大国主命、事代主命 ** 例祭：11月19日 * 祓戸神社 ** 祭神：祓戸四神 ** 例祭：5月5日 * 静霊神社 ** 祭神：島田市出身の国事殉難者1950余柱 ** 例祭：4月15日 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Kasuga-jinja & Tenmangu.JPG|大井天満宮（右）と春日神社（左） File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Ebisu-jinja.JPG|大井恵比寿神社 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Haraedo-jinja.JPG|祓戸神社 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Shizutama-jinja.JPG|静霊神社 == 祭事 == === 年間祭事 === 大井神社で年間に行われる主な祭事の一覧[http://www.ooijinjya.org/events/index.html 年中行事]（公式サイト）。。 * 歳旦祭 （1月1日） * 節分祭 （2月3日） * 雛人形焼納祭 （4月7日） * 春日神社例祭 （6月5日） * みそぎの祓い （6月30日） * 夏祭り、七夕祭り （8月1日） * '''例祭''' （10月15日） * 恵比寿神社例祭、恵比寿講社祭 （11月19日） * 鎮火祭 （12月1日） === 島田大祭 === 大井神社の祭は[[島田大祭]]（帯まつり）と称される。[[元禄]]8年（1695年）に始まると伝えられ、現在は3年に1度（寅年・巳年・申年・亥年）の10月中旬の3日間に行われる。 帯祭りでは、特に最終日の神輿渡御神事の際の大名行列が知られる。この行列は元禄絵巻さながらのもので、大奴が安産祈願の帯を木太刀に下げて練り歩く（「帯祭り」の由来）ほか、疫病退散を願う[[鹿島踊り]]、子供が[[長唄]]に合わせて[[歌舞伎]]踊りを披露する[[山車|屋台]]等が付く。祭りの間、島田市の中心部は7つの街（がい）に分けられ、1-5街では屋台を中心とした長唄祭り、6街では鹿島踊り、7街では大名行列と大奴の練り歩きを行う。 『年中行事辞典』は[[山梨県]][[富士吉田市]]の[[吉田の火祭]]、島田大祭、[[愛知県]][[稲沢市]]の[[国府宮はだか祭]]を「日本三奇祭」としている『第二回企画展 島田大祭展』島田市博物館、1992年、p.6。また大名行列「島田帯祭の大名行列」として、鹿島踊りは「島田鹿島踊」としてそれぞれ静岡県指定無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 == 文化財 == === 静岡県指定文化財 === * 無形民俗文化財 ** 島田鹿島踊 - 大井神社ほか。1957年（昭和32年）5月13日指定[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/kashima.html 島田鹿島踊]（島田市博物館ホームページ）。。 ** 島田帯祭の大名行列 - 大井神社ほか。1996年（平成8年）3月12日指定[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/obimatsuri-daimyou.html 島田帯祭の大名行列]（島田市博物館ホームページ）。。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[静岡県]][[島田市]]大井町2316 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：[[東海旅客鉄道]]（JR東海）[[東海道本線]] [[島田駅 (静岡県)|島田駅]] （徒歩約5分） '''周辺''' * [[島田宿|島田宿大井川川越遺跡]] - 国の史跡。 * [[蓬萊橋 (大井川)|蓬萊橋]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=大井神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/885}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）885-886コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|島田市史 上|1978年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=野本寛一|authorlink=野本寛一|year=1987|chapter=大井神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=[[白水社]]|isbn=4560022208|ref={{Harvid|大井神社（神々）|1987年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=島田宿|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|島田宿（平凡社）|2000年}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} * [http://www.ooijinjya.org/ 大井神社] - Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[:en:Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]] Oimachi 2316 |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|1.18|N|138|10|18.40|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]]
[[:en:Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|Haniyasu]]
[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}
[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] |founded = Unknown |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |alternate_names = |offices = |annual_festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] |rituals = {{ill|Shimada Grand festival|ja|島田大祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Grand festival|qq=|1=Shimada Taisai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48764792}} (Held every three years) }} [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Oi Shrine''' (大井神社, Oi-jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is designated as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and currently a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine is dedicated to the spirit of the [[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]] and is famous for the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り) held every three years. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities:Shrine history. * [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]] (Mizuhanome no Kami) - deity of water. * [[:en:Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|Haniyasu]] (Haniyasu Hime no Kami) - deity of earth. * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] (Amaterasu Sume Ohmikami) - deity of the sun. In historical texts, the shrine is referred to as "Oi no Kami," indicating its origins in the deification of the [[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]]. The three deities mentioned above were enshrined later, particularly Mizuhanome, who reflects the shrine's original connection to water.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} == History == [[File:Oigawa Shimada.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]] (Ancient Ritual Site)}}]] [[File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] == Overview == The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown. Historical records indicate that in 865 (Jōgan 7), the deity of "Oi no Kami" in [[:en:Suruga Province|Suruga Province]] was elevated to the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. However, the shrine is not listed in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] records, making it a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (a shrine not listed in the Engishiki). The {{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga no Kuni Shingi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} records indicate that "Oi Tenjin" had a divine Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} According to tradition, the shrine was originally located in the upper reaches of the [[:en:Ōi River|Oigawa River]] in the village of Yahatamura (now part of [[:en:Kawanehon|Kawanehon, Shizuoka]]) but was swept away by a flood in 1276 (Kenji 2) and drifted to Shimada, where it has since been enshrined at its current location.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} A monument now marks the original site of the shrine in the Yahatamura area, although there are several other locations with similar claims.[http://www.ooijinjya.org/knowledge/knowledge03.html Knowledge about the Oi Shrine] (Official website). Due to the frequent flooding of the Oigawa River, over 50 branch shrines of "Oi Shrine" have been established throughout the region.{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|1978th year }}}} During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was temporarily relocated to the Gotemizutani area of Nodamura in 1604 (Keicho 9) due to the collapse of the Oigawa River embankment, but it was returned to its original location in 1615 (Genna 1).{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} In 1689 (Genroku 2), the shrine was relocated to its current site due to the development of [[:en:Shimada-juku|Shimada-juku]].{{Cite book|和書|editor=島田市史編纂委員会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref={{Harvid|Shimada City History|2000th year }}}}{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} The shrine received a small donation of land from [[:en:Imagawa Ujizane|Imagawa Ujizane]], and during the [[:en:Tokugawa clan|Tokugawa]] period, it had an exemption of 5 bushels and 9 ho.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }}}} After the [[Meiji Restoration]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1875 and promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1908. In 1966 (Showa 41), it was designated a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. === Divine Rankings === * Historical records during the Six National Histories period ** 865 (Jōgan 7) December 21, elevated from Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ([[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]) - Recorded as "Oi no Kami." * Post-Six National Histories ** Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade ({{ill|Suruga no Kuni Shingi|ja|駿河国内神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga no Kuni Shingi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) - Recorded as "Oi Tenjin." == Sub-Shrines == The shrine grounds contain the following five sub-shrines: * Oi Tenmangu ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} ** festival: October 25 * Kasuga Shrine ** Deities: Takemikazuchi no Mikoto, Futsunushi no Mikoto, and nine others ** festival: Early June * Oi Ebisu Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} ** festival: November 19 * Haraedo Shrine ** Deities: Haraedo no Ōkami (four deities) ** festival: May 5 * Shizutama Shrine ** Deities: Over 1950 martyrs from Shimada ** festival: April 15 File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Kasuga-jinja & Tenmangu.JPG|Oi Tenmangu (right) and Kasuga Shrine (left) File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Ebisu-jinja.JPG|Oi Ebisu Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Haraedo-jinja.JPG|Haraedo Shrine File:Oi-jinja (Shimada) Shizutama-jinja.JPG|Shizutama Shrine == annual Events == === festivals === The major festivals held at Oi Shrine include:[http://www.ooijinjya.org/events/index.html annual Events] (Official website). * Saitan-sai (January 1) * Setsubun-sai (February 3) * Hina Doll Burning festival (April 7) * Kasuga Shrine festival (June 5) * Misogi no Harae (June 30) * Summer festival, Tanabata festival (August 1) * festival (October 15) * Ebisu Shrine festival, Ebisu Kosha festival (November 19) * Chinka-sai (December 1) === Shimada Grand festival === The shrine's festival is known as the [[Shimada Grand festival|Shimada Grand festival]] (帯祭り). It is said to have started in 1695 (Genroku 8) and is held every three years (years of the Tiger, Snake, Monkey, and Boar) over three days in mid-October. The festival is particularly famous for the daimyo procession on the final day. This procession recreates the grandeur of the Genroku period, with retainers carrying a childbirth prayer sash (the origin of the festival's name) on wooden swords, along with the {{ill|Kashima Odori|ja|鹿島踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鹿島踊り|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}} dance for pestilence avoidance and kabuki dances by children on [[:en:Market stall|Yatai]] floats. During the festival, Shimada's central area is divided into seven districts, with different events held in each: nagauta (long song) festivals with floats in districts 1-5, Kashima Odori in district 6, and the daimyo procession and parade in district 7. The {{ill|Nenchū Gyōji Jiten|ja|年中行事辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nenchū Gyōji Jiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lists the [[Yoshida Fire Festival|Yoshida Fire festival]] in Yamanashi, the Shimada Grand festival, and the [[Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri|Konomiya Hadaka Matsuri]] in Aichi as the "Three Great Strange festivals of Japan." ‘Second Exhibition: Shimada Grand festival’, Shimada City Museum, 1992, p.6 Additionally, the daimyo procession of the Shimada Grand festival and the Kashima Odori dance are designated as Intangible Folk Cultural Properties by Shizuoka Prefecture. == Cultural Properties == === Shizuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ** {{ill|Shimada Kashima Odori|ja|島田鹿島踊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Kashima Odori|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on May 13, 1957.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/kashima.html Shimada Kashima Odori] (Shimada City Museum website). ** {{ill|Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|ja|島田帯祭の大名行列|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Oi Shrine and others. Designated on March 12, 1996.[https://www.city.shimada.shizuoka.jp/bunkazai/archives/ken/obimatsuri-daimyou.html Shimada Grand festival Daimyo Procession] (Shimada City Museum website). == location Information == '''location''' * Oimachi 2316, Shimada, Shizuoka Prefecture, Japan '''Access''' * Train: 5-minute walk from {{ill|Shimada Station|ja|島田駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada Station|qq=|1=Shimada Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11052398}} on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] of [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|Central Japan Railway Company]] (JR Tōkai) '''Nearby''' * {{ill|Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|ja|島田宿大井川川越遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimada-juku Oigawa Kawagoe Ruins|qq=|1=Shimada-shuku Ōi-gawa Kawagoshi Rerics|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48442779}} - National Historic Site. * {{ill|Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|ja|蓬萊橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|qq=|1=Hōrai Bridge (Oigawa)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621618}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine history * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=Meiji Shrine History|1912nd year }} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/885}} '府県郷社明治神社誌料 上'] (National Diet Library Digital Collections) See 885-886 frames. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1978|chapter=|title=島田市史 上巻|publisher=島田市|isbn=|ref=Shimada City History|1978th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=野本寛一|authorlink=野本寛一|year=1987|chapter=Oi Shrine|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=白水社|isbn=4560022208|ref=Oi Shrine (Gods)|1987th year }} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2000|chapter=Shimada-juku|title=日本歴史地名大系 22 静岡県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490220|ref=Shimada-juku (Heibonsha)|2000th year }} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Oi-jinja (Shimada)}} * [http://www.ooijinjya.org/ Oi Shrine] - Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280}} |location = [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[:en:Shimada, Shizuoka|Shimada]] Oimachi 2316 |coordinates = {{coord|34|50|1.18|N|138|10|18.40|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]]
